title: Action cards replace raw markdown handoff surfaces
qaNote: All 6 ACs verified: no top-level <pre> in onboarding.tsx; ActionCard exported with correct props; <details> collapsed by default with focus rings; 1800 ms copied state and all three payload headers confirmed; PhaseFirst renders ActionCard (no HandoffDock); HandoffDock.tsx unmodified.

impl.notes: New shared component ActionCard (work-hq/src/components/work-hq/ActionCard.tsx): props title, meta?, stepList, copyPayload, statusLine?, copyLabel?, hint?, className?. Leads with an always-visible ordered step summary, a prominent Copy button (~1.8s copied-state via setTimeout 1800), an optional statusLine slot, and the raw markdown inside a native <details><summary>View raw prompt</summary>…</details> collapsed by default (no `open` attr). Token-only styling matching HandoffDock (rounded-2xl, border-primary/25, bg-primary/5, shadow-[0_0_40px_-20px_var(--primary)]); WCAG 2.2 AA focus rings (focus-visible ring-ring + ring-offset-background) on both the Copy button and the summary; the step-number badges are aria-hidden. The three onboarding surfaces now render ActionCard: BootstrapCopyBlock and ProfileCorrectionsDock became thin ActionCard wrappers, and PhaseFirst (phase 6) builds its markdown via buildHandoff and passes it as copyPayload (HandoffDock import removed from onboarding.tsx). Clipboard payloads are byte-identical — readBootstrapPrompt output, buildProfileCorrectionsMarkdown, and buildHandoff are all unchanged and passed straight through. Phase-3 profile-detection polling (waiting → detected) was consolidated into the ActionCard statusLine; the standalone waiting block was removed as it duplicated that status. The unused Copy lucide import was dropped from onboarding.tsx. HandoffDock.tsx is untouched (git diff empty) and remains in use by four other routes. NOTE for QA: AC4's stated bootstrap-prompt first line ('## Work HQ handoff · profile-bootstrap …') does not match the frozen §4.2 contract — the bootstrap payload is the raw template content (readBootstrapPrompt), which was NOT changed per outOfScope/technicalNotes. The profile-corrections and onboarding payloads do begin with their '## Work HQ handoff · <kind> · <date>' headers, verified unchanged.

verificationRun.result: PASS — zero matches; no top-level <pre> in the route. The only <pre> now lives inside ActionCard's <details> disclosure (ActionCard.tsx).
PASS — PhaseFirst renders <ActionCard>; grep 'HandoffDock' in onboarding.tsx returns 0 matches.
PASS — ✓ built in 383ms; onboarding chunk 80.77 kB gzip 17.89 kB; re-verified clean after prettier.
PASS — ActionCard.tsx reformatted (line wrap); onboarding.tsx unchanged.

userStory: As a user completing onboarding, I want the three handoff surfaces to present a human-readable summary and a single Copy button, so that I never see raw markdown as primary UI content.

acceptanceCriteria.text: No `<pre>` element displaying markdown content is visible by default anywhere in the onboarding route. After implementation, loading `/onboarding` in a browser and inspecting the DOM shows no top-level `<pre>` element outside a collapsed disclosure. `grep -n '<pre' work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx` returns only lines that fall inside an ActionCard disclosure block.

`work-hq/src/components/work-hq/ActionCard.tsx` exists and exports `ActionCard`. The component accepts at minimum: `stepList: string[]`, `copyPayload: string`, and optional `statusLine?: React.ReactNode`. `grep -n 'export.*ActionCard' work-hq/src/components/work-hq/ActionCard.tsx` returns a match.

ActionCard includes a 'View raw prompt' disclosure — HTML `<details>`/`<summary>` or equivalent ARIA pattern — that is collapsed by default (the `open` attribute is absent on first render). Clicking it expands to show the full markdown payload; clicking again collapses it. `grep -n 'details\|disclosure\|View raw' work-hq/src/components/work-hq/ActionCard.tsx` returns a match.

The Copy button transitions to a 'Copied' success state for approximately 1.8 s after click, matching the timing from the replaced components. The profile-corrections ActionCard payload begins exactly `## Work HQ handoff · profile-corrections · <YYYY-MM-DD>` and the phase-6 onboarding ActionCard payload begins exactly `## Work HQ handoff · onboarding · <YYYY-MM-DD>` (docs/specs/file-contracts.md §4.3 and §4.1). The bootstrap-prompt ActionCard payload is byte-identical to the output of `readBootstrapPrompt` per §4.2 — it is NOT a `buildHandoff` block; verify by clicking Copy in phase 3 and confirming the pasted payload begins `# Bootstrap Profile Prompt` (raw substituted template content, not a `## Work HQ handoff` header line).

Phase 6 component (`PhaseFirst`) renders `ActionCard` instead of `HandoffDock`. `grep -n 'ActionCard' work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx` returns at least one match whose line number falls inside the `PhaseFirst` function body. `grep -n 'HandoffDock' work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx` returns no match whose line number falls inside the `PhaseFirst` function body.

The generic `HandoffDock` component file (`work-hq/src/components/work-hq/HandoffDock.tsx`) is unmodified by this story. After implementation, `git diff HEAD -- work-hq/src/components/work-hq/HandoffDock.tsx` returns no output.

technicalNotes: Three surfaces to replace in `work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx`: (1) `BootstrapCopyBlock` (lines ~521–578) — currently renders a `<pre>` with the first line highlighted in `<code>` then raw content; (2) `ProfileCorrectionsDock` (lines ~632–703) — same `<pre>` pattern; (3) `PhaseFirst` HandoffDock usage (line ~2218). Create `ActionCard` as a new shared component at `work-hq/src/components/work-hq/ActionCard.tsx`. ActionCard renders: (a) `stepList` as a short human-readable bullet list of what the assistant will do — always visible above the Copy button; (b) a prominent Copy button with 1.8 s copied-state feedback; (c) an optional `statusLine` ReactNode slot for polling-based feedback (e.g. '✓ Profile detected'); (d) the full `copyPayload` markdown inside a `<details><summary>View raw prompt</summary>…</details>` block, collapsed by default. The `buildProfileCorrectionsMarkdown` function (lines ~589–626) and `readBootstrapPrompt` server function remain unchanged — their output is passed unchanged as `copyPayload` to ActionCard. The phase-6 HandoffDock spec is also passed through ActionCard's `copyPayload` using the existing `buildHandoff` utility from `work-hq/src/lib/handoff.ts`. Do not modify `HandoffDock.tsx`.
outOfScope: Changing the handoff payload content, header format, or markdown structure (docs/specs/file-contracts.md §4 is frozen). Applying ActionCard to any route other than /onboarding. Modifying HandoffDock.tsx or any other route component.

verificationPlan.checks: Every match falls inside the ActionCard disclosure block (within a `<details>` element). Zero top-level `<pre>` elements render by default.
Returns a match confirming the component is exported from the expected path.
Returns no output — HandoffDock.tsx is unmodified by this story.

revisionLog.change: AC4 corrected: removed erroneous '## Work HQ handoff · profile-bootstrap' first-line assertion for the bootstrap payload; per file-contracts.md §4.2 the bootstrap payload is raw substituted template content (begins '# Bootstrap Profile Prompt'), not a buildHandoff block — the profile-corrections and onboarding payload header assertions are unchanged.
revisionLog.reason: ui-dev flagged spec/contract mismatch: implementation correctly preserved the byte-identical bootstrap payload per §4.2 and story outOfScope, but AC4 had asserted an incorrect first-line format for that payload.

perAc.ac: AC1 — No top-level <pre> visible by default in the onboarding route
perAc.evidence: ran `grep -n '<pre' work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx` → no output (zero matches). The sole <pre> in the route's component tree lives inside ActionCard.tsx line 129, which is nested inside the <details> disclosure (line 124-133) and is therefore collapsed by default.

perAc.ac: AC2 — ActionCard.tsx exists, exports ActionCard, accepts stepList, copyPayload, statusLine
perAc.evidence: `grep -n 'export.*ActionCard' work-hq/src/components/work-hq/ActionCard.tsx` → line 19: `export function ActionCard({`. Props inspected: `stepList: string[]` (line 34), `copyPayload: string` (line 35), `statusLine?: ReactNode` (line 38). File read in full at work-hq/src/components/work-hq/ActionCard.tsx.

perAc.ac: AC3 — 'View raw prompt' <details>/<summary> disclosure, collapsed by default, keyboard-operable, focus-visible rings on summary and copy button
perAc.evidence: `grep -n 'details\|disclosure\|View raw' work-hq/src/components/work-hq/ActionCard.tsx` → lines 11, 37, 41, 124, 127, 133. ActionCard.tsx line 124: `<details className="group mt-4">` — no `open` attribute. Line 125: `<summary ...>View raw prompt</summary>` with `focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring focus-visible:ring-offset-2 focus-visible:ring-offset-background`. Copy button (line 76) carries identical focus-visible ring classes. Native <details>/<summary> is keyboard-operable (Enter/Space) without additional ARIA. Step-number badges are aria-hidden (line 109).

perAc.ac: AC4 — 1.8 s copied state; profile-corrections payload begins ## Work HQ handoff · profile-corrections · <date>; onboarding payload begins ## Work HQ handoff · onboarding · <date>; bootstrap payload begins # Bootstrap Profile Prompt
perAc.evidence: ActionCard.tsx line 51: `setTimeout(() => setCopied(false), 1800)` — 1800 ms = 1.8 s. onboarding.tsx line 560: `const header = \`## Work HQ handoff · profile-corrections · ${today}\`` in buildProfileCorrectionsMarkdown. handoff.ts line 26: `const header = \`## Work HQ handoff · ${kind} · ${today()}\`` with kind='onboarding'. templates/bootstrap-profile-prompt.md first line: `# Bootstrap Profile Prompt · Updated: 2026-07-05` (begins '# Bootstrap Profile Prompt'). `git diff bd1ed70~1 bd1ed70 -- work-hq/src/lib/handoff.ts work-hq/src/server/read-bootstrap-prompt.ts` → empty (both unchanged). BootstrapCopyBlock passes `content` prop directly as `copyPayload={content}` (diff line +531); ActionCard writes `copyPayload` verbatim to clipboard (ActionCard.tsx line 48).

perAc.ac: AC5 — PhaseFirst renders ActionCard (not HandoffDock); grep ActionCard returns match inside PhaseFirst; grep HandoffDock returns no match inside PhaseFirst
perAc.evidence: `grep -n 'ActionCard\|HandoffDock' work-hq/src/routes/onboarding.tsx` → ActionCard matched at lines 11 (import), 516, 530, 594, 610, 862, 2116, 2145; HandoffDock NOT present in file at all (zero matches). PhaseFirst function starts at line 2114; `<ActionCard` at line 2145 is inside its body (function closes at line 2157).

perAc.ac: AC6 — HandoffDock.tsx unmodified by this story
perAc.evidence: `git diff bd1ed70~1 bd1ed70 -- work-hq/src/components/work-hq/HandoffDock.tsx` → empty. `git diff HEAD -- work-hq/src/components/work-hq/HandoffDock.tsx` → empty.

qaReport.securityAndMigrationGates: Secrets scan: PASS — `git show bd1ed70 | grep -iE '(DATABASE_URL|API_KEY|SECRET|TOKEN|PASSWORD|PRIVATE.?KEY|BEGIN RSA|eyJ)'` returned two CSS design-token comment lines only; no credential or secret material present. Migration check: N/A — no database schema changes. Only files modified are ActionCard.tsx (new component), onboarding.tsx (route refactor), and 012.json (story metadata).
qaReport.riskNotes: 1. Runtime clipboard verification not performed — no headless browser harness available. Payload byte-identity is confirmed by static analysis: (a) handoff.ts and read-bootstrap-prompt.ts are both unchanged (empty diff); (b) BootstrapCopyBlock passes its content prop directly as copyPayload; (c) ProfileCorrectionsDock passes buildProfileCorrectionsMarkdown output directly as copyPayload; (d) PhaseFirst passes buildHandoff output directly as copyPayload; (e) ActionCard writes copyPayload verbatim to clipboard (line 48). 2. buildHandoff's today() is evaluated at component render time, not at copy time. If the user leaves phase 6 open across midnight the date in the copied block is the previous day. This is a pre-existing limitation of buildHandoff used identically in HandoffDock across all other routes and is outside story 012's scope. 3. group-open:rotate-90 Tailwind utility on ChevronRight depends on the Tailwind v3+ group-open variant — confirmed functional since the production build succeeds with no CSS warnings.
qaReport.recommendedFollowUps: 1. Follow-ups pinned to story 012 from earlier stories: none identified. Story 011's follow-ups were pinned to story 006 (already Done/Approved); story 006's QA report carried three items (scaffold-files integration test, org.md E2E write, profile.md change-between-loads) as REQUIRES NEW STORY — none reference story 012 and none are resolved by this story's scope. These remain open and require a dedicated test/maintenance story under f-onboarding-ux or a new feature. 2. FUTURE: Consider moving buildHandoff's today() call into the copy handler so the date stamp is captured at copy time rather than render time — closes the midnight edge case across all HandoffDock and ActionCard surfaces.
